history: The client has been experiencing significant mood instability and anxiety, with depressive episodes becoming more frequent and severe. She has a history of being pampered by her parents, leading to fewer coping mechanisms for dealing with adversity. Ongoing conflicts with authority figures contribute to her stress levels. She relies on medication to manage her anxiety and mood instability.
intermediate_belief: If I can't meet expectations, then I am a failure and will never succeed.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't meet expectations, then I will never get better and will always be stuck in this state.
coping_strategies: The client relies on medication to manage her anxiety and mood instability. She experiences a cycle of 'rev and crash,' indicating a possible bipolar component. She also engages in reading, which provides some pleasure and distraction.
situation: Encouraging students to socialize and participate in campus activities, with results falling short of expectations.
auto_thought: I can't do this; I'm failing at my job.
behavior: She becomes irritable and struggles to focus, spending time looking for things due to forgetfulness.
